Which two signs should be interchanged to make the equation correct? 20 ÷ 4 X 2 - 5 + 3 = 38
questions.explanation: Option (a) If ÷ and + signs are interchanged, then the equation will be: 20 + 4 X 2 - 5 ÷ 3 = 38 20 + 8 - 1.66 = 38 28 - 1.66 = 38 26.34 ≠ 38 Thus, the equation is not balanced. Option (b) If ÷ and - signs are interchanged, then the equation will be: 20 - 4 X 2 ÷ 5 + 3 = 38 20 - 4 x 0.4 + 3 = 38 20 - 1.6 + 3 = 38 23 - 1.6 = 38 -21.4 ≠ 38 Thus, the equation is not balanced. Option (c) If + and x signs are interchanged, then the equation will be: 20 ÷ 4 + 2 - 5 x 3 = 38 5 + 2 - 15 = 38 7 - 15 = 38 -8 ≠ 38 Thus, the equation is not balanced. Option (d) If ÷ and x signs are interchanged, then the equation will be: 20 x 4 ÷ 2 - 5 + 3 = 38 20 x 2 - 5 + 3 = 38 40 - 5 + 3 = 38 43 - 5 = 38 38 = 38 Thus, the equation is balanced. So, option (d) is correct.

questions.explanation: Statements: All Saw is Axe Few Saw is Knife means some Saw is Knife No Knife is Chopper ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23324/image019.png) Some Axe is Knife: All Saw is Axe. Few Saw is Knife means some Saw is Knife. Thus, some Axe is Knife. So, conclusion I is true. No Axe is Chopper: As there is no direct relation between Axe and Chopper. So, conclusion II is false. Hence, only conclusions I and III follow.

questions.question: Who among the following is known for his autobiography 'Akkarmashi' published in 1984?
questions.explanation: Sharankumar Limbale is a Marathi language author, poet and literary critic. He has penned more than 40 books. His best known work is his autobiography Akkarmashi published in 1984. Akkarmashi has been translated to several other Indian languages and English. The English translation has been published by the Oxford University Press with the title The Outcaste. In news: Marathi writer and critic Sarankumar Limbale has been selected for the 2025 Chintha Raveendran Award. The award recognises his significant contributions to literature, especially his efforts in advancing Dalit identity, equality, and social justice through his writings. The award includes a cash prize of ₹50,000, a memento, and a citation. The presentation ceremony will be held on July 26 at K.P. Kesavamenon Hall in Kozhikode, Kerala. The Chintha Raveendran Award is presented annually to a writer or intellectual who has made impactful contributions to literature and social discourse in India. It is named after Chintha Raveendran, a noted leftist thinker and writer, and honours voices that stand for equality, justice, and social reform.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is/are true about Mohiniyattam? A. Mohiniyattam is characterized by abrupt jerks or sudden leaps. B. It belongs to the lasya style which is feminine, tender and graceful. C. Movements have been borrowed from Nangiar Koothu and female folk dances Kaikottikali and the Tiruvatirakali.
questions.explanation: Salient Features of Mohiniyattam Dance: Mohiniyattam is characterized by graceful, swaying body movements with no abrupt jerks or sudden leaps. It belongs to the lasya style which is feminine, tender and graceful. The movements are emphasized by the glides and the up and down movement on toes, like the waves of the sea and the swaying of the coconut, palm trees and the paddy fields. The foot work is not terse and is rendered softly. Importance is given to the hand gestures and Mukhabhinaya with subtle facial expressions. Movements have been borrowed from Nangiar Koothu and female folk dances Kaikottikali and the Tiruvatirakali. Mohiniyattam lays emphasis on acting. The dancer identifies herself with the character and sentiments existing in the compositions like the Padams and Pada Varnams which give ample opportunity for facial expressions. The hand gestures, 24 in number, are mainly adopted from Hastalakshana Deepika, a text followed by Kathakali. Few are also borrowed from NatyaShastra, AbhinayaDarpana and Balarambharatam. The gestures and facial expressions are closer to the natural (gramya) and the realistic (lokadharmi) than to the dramatic or rigidly conventional (natyadharmi). The traditional repertoire includes Chollukettu, Jatiswaram, Padavarnam, Padam,Tillana and Slokam. Besides these Pandattam and Omanatinkal (lullaby), introduced by Vallatol are also popular and are often included in a recital. Most of the compositions included in the repertoire have been composed by Swati Tirunal which emphasizes the Sahitya Bhava i.e. the literary content. These are portrayed through appropriate hand and facial expressions that include the nine sentiments.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020: I.The NEP 2020 restructures the school curriculum and pedagogical structure from the existing 10+2 system to a new 5+3+3+4 design. II.The policy proposes a single regulatory body, the Higher Education Commission of India (HECI), to govern all higher education institutions, including medical and legal education. III.A key target of the NEP 2020 is to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by the year 2030. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
options: I, II and III are correct
questions.explanation: National Education Policy(NEP) 2020 was announced on 29.07.2020. Statement II is incorrect: Policy suggests a single overarching umbrella body for promotion of higher education sector including teacher education and excluding medical and legal education- the Higher Education Commission of India (HECI). Statement III is incorrect: NEP, 2020 aim to increase the GER to 100% in preschool to secondary level by 2030 whereas GER in Higher Education including vocational education from 26.3% (2018) to 50% by 2035. Details of some of salient features of NEP 2020 are as follows- - Ensuring Universal Access at All Levels of schooling from pre-primary school to Grade 12; - Ensuring quality early childhood care and education for all children between 3-6 years; - New Curricular and Pedagogical Structure (5+3+3+4); - No hard separations between arts and sciences, between curricular and extra-curricular activities, between vocational and academic streams; - Establishing National Mission on Foundational Literacy and Numeracy; - Emphasis on promoting multilingualism and Indian languages; The medium of instruction until at least Grade 5, but preferably till Grade 8 and beyond, will be the home language/mother tongue/local language/regional language. - Assessment reforms - Board Exams on up to two occasions during any given school year, one main examination and one for improvement, if desired; - Setting up of a new National Assessment Centre, PARAKH (Performance Assessment, Review, and Analysis of Knowledge for Holistic Development); - Equitable and inclusive education - Special emphasis given on Socially and Economically Disadvantaged Groups (SEDGs); - A separate Gender Inclusion fund and Special Education Zones for disadvantaged regions and groups; - Robust and transparent processes for recruitment of teachers and merit based performance; - Ensuring availability of all resources through school complexes and clusters; - Setting up of State School Standards Authority (SSSA); - Exposure of vocational education in school and higher education system; - Increasing GER in higher education to 50%; - Holistic and Multidisciplinary Education with multiple entry/exit options; - NTA to offer Common Entrance Exam for Admission to HEIs; - Establishment of Academic Bank of Credit; - Setting up of Multidisciplinary Education and Research Universities (MERUs); - Setting up of National Research Foundation (NRF); - 'Light but Tight' regulation; - Single overarching umbrella body for promotion of higher education sector including teacher education and excluding medical and legal education- the Higher Education Commission of India (HECI)-with independent bodies for standard setting- the General Education Council; funding-Higher Education Grants Council (HEGC); accreditation- National Accreditation Council (NAC); and regulation- National Higher Education Regulatory Council (NHERC); - Expansion of open and distance learning to increase Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER). - Internationalization of Education - Professional Education will be an integral part of the higher education system. Stand-alone technical universities, health science universities, legal and agricultural universities, or institutions in these or other fields, will aim to become multi-disciplinary institutions. - Teacher Education - 4-year integrated stage-specific, subject- specific Bachelor of Education - Establishing a National Mission for Mentoring. - Creation of an autonomous body, the National Educational Technology Forum (NETF) to provide a platform for the free exchange of ideas on the use of technology to enhance learning, assessment, planning, administration. Appropriate integration of technology into all levels of education. - Achieving 100% youth and adult literacy. - Multiple mechanisms with checks and balances will combat and stop the commercialization of higher education. - All education institutions will be held to similar standards of audit and disclosure as a not for profit entity. - The Centre and the States will work together to increase the public investment in Education sector to reach 6% of GDP at the earliest. - Strengthening of the Central Advisory Board of Education to ensure coordination to bring overall focus on quality education. - NEP, 2020 aim to increase the GER to 100% in preschool to secondary level by 2030 whereas GER in Higher Education including vocational education from 26.3% (2018) to 50% by 2035.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Gupta Dynasty: a. The achievements of Samudragupta are recorded in the Prayag Prashasti (Allahabad Pillar Inscription). b. The Gupta rulers were primary patrons of Jainism and made it the state religion. c. The Chinese Buddhist pilgrim Fa-Hien (Faxian) visited India during the reign of Chandragupta II. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement a is correct. The Prayag Prashasti, an inscription on the Allahabad Pillar, was composed by Samudragupta's court poet, Harisena. It details the military conquests and achievements of Samudragupta, famously referring to him as the "Napoleon of India." Statement b is incorrect. The Gupta rulers were predominantly followers of Vaishnavism (a sect of Hinduism), not Jainism. While they were tolerant of other religions like Buddhism and Jainism, Vaishnavism received the most royal patronage. Statement c is correct. The Chinese Buddhist monk Fa-Hien traveled to India in search of Buddhist scriptures and spent several years in the Gupta Empire during the reign of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya), providing a valuable historical account of the period.

questions.question: When fatty acids are attached to a Glycerol-3-Phosphate backbone, which molecule is produced as the final storage form of energy in adipose tissue?
questions.explanation: Triglycerides (also called triacylglycerols) are formed when: · Three fatty acid molecules esterify with one glycerol (via glycerol-3-phosphate) · They are the primary energy storage molecules in adipose tissue. · Yield more than twice the energy per gram compared to carbohydrates. Phospholipids contain: Two fatty acids + glycerol + phosphate group They are structural components of cell membranes, not energy storage molecules. Cholesterol is a sterol, It plays roles in: · Membrane fluidity · Precursor of steroid hormones, bile acids, vitamin D Sphingolipids are built on a sphingosine backbone, not glycerol. They are mainly involved in: · Myelin sheath · Cell recognition and signaling Further Insights · Triglycerides are stored in adipocytes as fat droplets. · They provide long-term energy storage. · Oxidation of triglycerides yields ATP, water, and CO₂. · Glycerol-3-phosphate links carbohydrate and lipid metabolism. · Excess carbohydrates are converted into triglycerides via lipogenesis.

questions.question: Which census introduced the provisions for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in India?
questions.explanation: Since 1951, independent India's census only counted the castes of Dalits and Adivasis, referred to as scheduled castes and tribes respectively. Everyone else's caste was marked as general. From 1950, India has reserved government jobs, college admissions and elected seats - from village councils to the parliament - for Dalits and Adivasis to rectify historical wrongs that have denied them equal opportunities.

questions.question: With reference to the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral and Technical Cooperation (BIMSTEC), consider the following statements: I. The 6th Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral and Technical Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Summit was held in Colombo in April, 2025. II. The theme of the 6th Summit was 'BIMSTEC - Prosperous, Resilient and Open'. III. The BIMSTEC grouping is a regional bloc comprising seven member states, five from South Asia and two from Southeast Asia. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi proposed an action plan while addressing the 6th Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral and Technical Cooperation (BIMSTEC) Summit in Bangkok on April 4. The summit was hosted by Thai Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra. This was the first physical meeting of the BIMSTEC leaders since the 4th BIMSTEC Summit in Kathmandu in 2018. The 5th BIMSTEC Summit was held at Colombo in March 2022 in virtual format. The theme of the 6th Summit was 'BIMSTEC - Prosperous, Resilient and Open'. The summit was attended by PM Modi, Nepal Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli, Bhutan Prime Minister Tsering Tobgay, Bangladesh chief advisor Muhammad Yunus and Myanmar Senior General Min Aung Hlaing. The summit adopted the Bangkok Vision 2030 and a Maritime Transportation Agreement to boost connectivity and engagement between member nations. The chairmanship of BIMSTEC was handed over to Bangladesh. Further insights: The Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) comprises India, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Bhutan and Nepal. It is a regional bloc comprising seven countries lying in the littoral and adjacent areas of the Bay of Bengal constituting a contiguous regional unity. This sub-regional organisation came into being on June 6, 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration. It constitutes five countries from South Asia (Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal and Sri Lanka) and two from Southeast Asia (Myanmar and Thailand). Initially, the economic bloc was formed with four member states with the acronym BIST-EC (Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka and Thailand Economic Cooperation). Following the inclusion of Myanmar on December 22, 1997 during a special ministerial meeting in Bangkok, the group was renamed BIMSTEC (Bangladesh, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka and Thailand Economic Cooperation). With the admission of Nepal and Bhutan at the sixth ministerial meeting in February 2004, the name of the grouping was changed to Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC). The BIMSTEC region is home to about 1.8 billion people, accounting for about 22 per cent of the global population with a combined GDP of $4.5 trillion. The BIMSTEC Secretariat is situated in Dhaka.

questions.question: Which of the following musical instruments is of Persian origin and became popular in India during the Mughal period?
questions.explanation: The Shehnai is a wind instrument believed to have originated from the Persian 'surnay'. It was introduced to India and gained immense popularity during the Mughal period, where it became an integral part of the naubat, or the traditional ensemble of nine instruments played at the royal court. It is considered an auspicious instrument and is traditionally played at temples and weddings. Explanation of Other Options: Sarod: While the Sarod evolved from the Afghan rubab, which has Persian roots, its modern form and popularity in Indian classical music developed in the 19th century, after the decline of the Mughal empire. Veena: This is an ancient string instrument with purely indigenous Indian origins, tracing back to Vedic times. It is not of Persian origin. Mridangam: This is an ancient double-headed percussion instrument from South India and is the primary rhythmic accompaniment in Carnatic music. It is of indigenous Indian origin. Further insights: The Shehnai was elevated from a folk/ceremonial instrument to a classical concert stage instrument largely due to the efforts of the legendary maestro, Ustad Bismillah Khan. The instrument is a quadruple-reed oboe, which gives it its distinct, powerful, and reedy tone.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the south-west Monsoon which is correct? A. It occurs from June to September B. It's Direction is north east to south west C. It given maximum rainfall as compared to other monsoon D. It has no branch
questions.explanation: Statements A and C are correct descriptions of the Southwest Monsoon in India. Explanation of Correct Statements: Statement A is correct: The Southwest Monsoon season is the principal rainy season for the Indian subcontinent, and its duration is officially recognized as the four-month period from June to September. Statement C is correct: The Southwest Monsoon accounts for approximately 75-80% of India's total annual rainfall, making it the season that provides the maximum rainfall compared to the retreating (Northeast) monsoon or pre-monsoon showers. Explanation of Incorrect Statements: Statement B is incorrect: The direction of the Southwest Monsoon winds is from south-west to north-east, as they blow from the sea towards the land. The direction mentioned (north-east to south-west) describes the Northeast Monsoon, or the retreating monsoon. Statement D is incorrect: A key feature of the Southwest Monsoon is that it splits into two main branches upon reaching the southern tip of the Indian Peninsula: The Arabian Sea branch moves northward along the west coast. The Bay of Bengal branch moves over the Bay of Bengal and is deflected westward up the Gangetic plains by the Himalayas. Further insights: The onset of the Southwest Monsoon is typically marked by its arrival on the Kerala coast around the first week of June. The withdrawal of the monsoon is a more gradual process, beginning from the northwestern states in mid-September and retreating completely from the peninsula by mid-October.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the game of chess: a. A 'stalemate' results in a win for the player whose king has no legal moves and is not in check. b. A pawn that reaches the farthest rank from its starting position must be promoted to a Queen, Knight, Rook, or Bishop of the same color. Which of the statements given above is/are incorrect?
questions.explanation: Statement a is incorrect. A stalemate occurs when a player, on their turn, has no legal moves, and their king is not in check. This situation does not result in a win; it results in a draw. Statement b is correct. This rule is called 'pawn promotion'. When a pawn advances to the eighth rank (the opponent's back rank), the player must immediately replace it with their choice of a Queen, Rook, Bishop, or Knight of the same color.

questions.question: Every Indian banknote bears a "Promissory Clause" signed by the Governor of the Reserve Bank of India, except for one specific denomination. Which of the following does NOT have this promise?
questions.explanation: The ₹1 note is printed by the Government, not RBI - so no RBI promise on it. The One Rupee Note is not issued by the Reserve Bank of India. It is issued by the Government of India and signed by the Finance Secretary, not the RBI Governor. Hence, it does not carry the RBI's promissory clause ("I promise to pay the bearer.."). Further Insights RBI issues currency notes from ₹2 onwards. The ₹1 note is a legal tender despite not being issued by RBI. Promissory clause reflects public trust in the monetary system. Coins in India do not carry a promissory clause. Signature on currency indicates the issuing authority.

questions.question: Consider the following two statements, labeled Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Evaluate them and choose the appropriate answer from the given options. Assertion (A): The boiling point of water decreases at higher altitudes. Reason (R): The atmospheric pressure is lower at higher altitudes.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. Based on the analysis of the statements: Assertion (A): The boiling point of water decreases at higher altitudes. This statement is true. At higher altitudes, the air is thinner, and the atmospheric pressure is lower. Reason (R): The atmospheric pressure is lower at higher altitudes. This statement is also true. Atmospheric pressure is the weight of the air column above a certain point; as altitude increases, this column of air becomes shorter and less dense, resulting in lower pressure. Relationship between A and R: The boiling point of a liquid is defined as the temperature at which its vapor pressure equals the surrounding atmospheric pressure. Since the atmospheric pressure is lower at higher altitudes (as stated in R), water needs to be heated to a lower temperature for its vapor pressure to equal this lower atmospheric pressure. Therefore, the Reason (R) is the correct scientific explanation for the Assertion (A).

questions.question: Identify the structural formula for ethene.
questions.explanation: Ethylene, also named ethene, has the formula C2H4 or H2C=CH2. It is the simplest alkene and also one of the most produced organic compounds in the world. questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about the Indus Valley Civilization? I) The Indus Valley Civilization was first discovered in Mohenjo-Daro. II) Sanskrit was the script used in this civilization.
options: I is correct, II is incorrect

questions.explanation: • The civilization was first identified in 1921 in Harappa by Dayaram Sahni, followed by Mohenjo-Daro in 1922. The Indus script used in the Indus Valley Civilization remains undeciphered to this day. • The Indus Valley Civilization, also known as the Harappan Civilization, is one of the world's earliest civilizations, established around 3300 BCE. The Indus Valley Civilization is characterized by its urban planning, elaborate drainage system, water supply systems, and baked brick houses. • Harappa and Mohenjo-daro were the major cities, while Kalibangan, Lothal, Chanhu-daro, Dholavira, and Banawali were the other cities. They worshipped both male and female deities, with Pashupati as the main male deity.

questions.question: Who among the following devised the Ryotwari system of land revenue collection in British India?
questions.explanation: • The Ryotwari system was introduced by Thomas Munro in Madras, Bombay, parts of Assam and Coorgh provinces of British India. • In the Ryotwari System the ownership rights were vested with the peasants. questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about the festival of Eid-ul-Fitr?
options: It is celebrated on a fixed date every year according to the solar calendar.
It is celebrated on the first day of Shawwal to mark the end of the fasting month of Ramadan.
It commemorates the first revelation of the Quran to Prophet Muhammad.
It is celebrated on the last day of Ramadan to begin the fasting period.
questions.explanation: The correct statement is that Eid-ul-Fitr is celebrated on the first day of Shawwal to mark the end of the fasting month of Ramadan. The name "Eid-ul-Fitr" translates to the "Festival of Breaking the Fast." It is a joyous occasion for Muslims worldwide to celebrate the successful completion of the month-long period of fasting, prayer, and reflection. Explanation of Other Options: (a) is incorrect: Like all Islamic festivals, Eid-ul-Fitr is based on the lunar calendar, so its date on the solar (Gregorian) calendar shifts by approximately 11 days each year. (c) is incorrect: The first revelation of the Quran is commemorated during Ramadan, specifically on Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Power), not on Eid-ul-Fitr. (d) is incorrect: Eid-ul-Fitr marks the end of the fasting period, not the beginning. It is celebrated after the month of Ramadan is over. Further insights: The celebration of Eid-ul-Fitr begins with the sighting of the crescent new moon, which signals the start of the month of Shawwal. Key traditions include special morning prayers (Salat al-Eid), giving charity to the needy (Zakat al-Fitr), wearing new clothes, visiting family and friends, and sharing festive meals.

questions.question: Match the following terms related to National Income Column 1Column 2 i. Net Domestic Producta. Domestic production + income from abroad ii. Gross National Productb. Market price - depreciation iii. Personal Incomec. GNP - depreciation iv. Net National Productd. National Income - undistributed profits - Net interest payments - Corporate tax + government transfer payments Options:
questions.explanation: • Net Domestic Product (NDP) = Market price - depreciation • Gross National Product (GNP) = Domestic production + income from abroad • Personal Income = National Income - undistributed profits - Net interest payments - Corporate tax + government transfer payments • Net National Product (NNP) = GNP minus depreciation Further Insight: • Private Income=National Income+Transfer Payments+Net Factor Income from Abroad+Interest on National Debt • Personal Income=Private Income−Undistributed Corporate Profits−Corporate Taxes−Social Security Contributions • GDP at factor cost = GDP at market prices - indirect taxes + subsidies • GNP=GDP+Net Factor Income from Abroad • Gross National Income (GNI)at market prices = GDP at market prices + taxes less subsidies on production and imports (Net receivable from abroad) + Compensation of Employees (Net receivable from abroad) + property income (Net receivable from abroad) • Net National Income (NNI) at market prices = GNI at market prices - consumption of fixed capital • Net National Disposable Income(NNDI) = NNI + net taxes on income and wealth receivable from abroad + net social contributions and benefits receivable from abroad

questions.question: If it's Aug-10 11:15am in Lucknow India, what is the time in Tokyo Japan?
questions.explanation: IST (Indian Standard Time) is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of GMT. So, 11:15 AM IST is 5:45 AM GMT. JST (Japan Standard Time) is 9 hours ahead of GMT. So, 5:45 AM GMT is 2:45 PM JST. Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the name for mean solar time of the longitude (0°) of the Royal Greenwich Observatory in England. The meridian at this longitude is called the prime meridian or Greenwich meridian. Greenwich Mean Time has been used to clearly designate epoch by avoiding confusing references to local time systems.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the nucleus is incorrect?
options: It contains the cell's genetic material, DNA.
It is surrounded by a double membrane called the nuclear envelope.
It is the site of protein synthesis.
The nucleolus within the nucleus is responsible for ribosome production.
questions.explanation: (a) It contains the cell's genetic material, DNA. ✅ Correct. The nucleus is the control center of the cell, containing DNA in the form of chromatin or chromosomes. (b) It is surrounded by a double membrane called the nuclear envelope. ✅ Correct. The nucleus is enclosed by a nuclear envelope, consisting of an inner and outer membrane. The outer membrane is continuous with the endoplasmic reticulum. (c) It is the site of protein synthesis. ❌ Incorrect. Protein synthesis does not take place inside the nucleus. The DNA in the nucleus provides the instructions for making proteins (transcription happens here → mRNA is made). However, actual protein synthesis occurs in ribosomes (either free in the cytoplasm or attached to rough endoplasmic reticulum). Hence, this statement is wrong. (d) The nucleolus within the nucleus is responsible for ribosome production. ✅ Correct. The nucleolus synthesizes rRNA and assembles ribosomal subunits, which later move to the cytoplasm for protein synthesis.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate pronoun to fill in the blank. The two rival debaters, after a heated argument on stage, surprisingly shook hands and wished ________ good luck for future competitions.
questions.explanation: The given sentence describes a moment of sportsmanship between two rivals. The action of "wishing good luck" is mutual or reciprocal between "two rival debaters." The blank needs a pronoun that expresses this mutual relationship. The correct option is "each other," which is a reciprocal pronoun used to indicate that an action is exchanged between two people or groups. The other options are incorrect. "Themselves" is a reflexive pronoun; "wished themselves good luck" would mean each person wished luck upon himself or herself, not upon the other. "Them" is an objective pronoun that would need a different sentence structure and would be less clear. "Their" is a possessive determiner that would need a noun to follow it (e.g., "wished for their success"), which is not present. Thus, the correct sentence is: "The two rival debaters, after a heated argument on stage, surprisingly shook hands and wished each other good luck for future competitions." Hence, option (b) is the answer. Note: Reciprocal pronouns express a mutual or reciprocal relationship and are considered as single units. Examples: Each other/ one another Reflexive Pronouns: Reflexive pronouns include myself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, and themselves. These are used to refer back to the subject of the clause in which they are used: She hurt herself in the eye. Objective Pronouns: The pronouns me, you, us, him, her, it, and them are called objective pronouns because they act as the objects of verbs and prepositions: Anjali drove us home. Possessive Determiners/Adjectives: Words like 'my', 'our', 'your', 'his', 'her', 'its', and 'their' are known as possessive adjectives (or possessive determiners). They come before nouns and indicate ownership of the noun. Example: "The teacher asked the students to open their drawing books."

questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Tense. There are three cases when the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Tense. · Tense remains same · Past Perfect changes to Present Perfect · Past Perfect changes to Simple Past Case 1: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 + Object) tense will be kept intact. For example: He said that he had just finished the movie. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I had just finished the movie" (Direct Speech) Case 2: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 +Object) will be converted into Present Perfect (Subject + has/have + V3 +Object). For example: He said that he had got a new job. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I have got a new job." (Direct Speech) Case 3: Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 + Object) will be converted into Past Simple (Subject + V2 + Object). For example: He said that Arya had sent him a book. (Indirect Speech) He said, "Arya sent me a book" (Direct Speech) Case 2 is applicable here: The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: "said to" changes to "told". "He" changes to "I". "had" changes to "have". "his" changes to "my". Therefore, the correct answer is: "He said to his friends, "I have finally finished writing my novel after working on it for over five years."" Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: Neither the manager nor the employees is happy with the decision.
Neither the manager nor the employees are happy with the decision.
Neither the manager or the employees is happy with the decision.
Neither the manager nor the employees has been happy with the decision.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (b). The verb "are" agrees with nearer plural subject "employees." Option (a) is incorrect because "is" is singular, but the nearer subject "employees" is plural, requiring plural "are." Option (c) is incorrect as "or" doesn't pair with "neither"; it must be "nor" for negative correlation. Option (d) is incorrect because "has" is singular, but the nearer subject "employees" is plural, requiring plural "are/have." Hence, the correct answer is option (b). Note: Subject-Verb Agreement: Matching up singular or plural subjects with singular or plural forms of a verb is part of the process called agreement. This can be understood better by the examples given: "She likes cooking." [singular subject and verb] -- "They like to cook vegetables." [plural subject and verb]

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined word in the given sentence. The patient was advised to walk onto the garden every morning.
questions.explanation: The preposition "onto" implies movement toward a surface (like a platform), which is incorrect for a space like a garden. Option d) in is correct - it refers to general presence within a space. Correct sentence: "The patient was advised to walk in the garden every morning." a) off implies movement away from something; incorrect. b) across implies a flat stretch - not garden-appropriate. c) into focuses on movement inside - less natural for a garden. Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in the positive degree of comparison. She is less friendly than her sister.
options: She is not closest to anyone like her sister.
Her sister is more friendly than she is.
She and her sister have the most friendliness in their nature.
Her sister is as distant as she.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in the comparative degree, which means it compares one thing or person with another and shows that it has a higher or lower degree of quality. The comparative degree is formed by using 'less' before an adjective. To change a sentence from the comparative degree to the positive degree, we need to remove 'less' before an adjective. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Options (a) and (c) are in superlative degree. Option (b) is in comparative degree. Note: Adjectives: Words which are used with a noun/pronoun to describe or point out, the person, animal, place, or thing which the noun names, or to tell the number or quantity, are called Adjectives. · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error in the usage of the interjection. She stood frozen / at the doorway and whispered, / "Yippee! There's someone / inside the house."
questions.explanation: "Yippee!" is an interjection used to express delight, excitement, or joy. In the given sentence, the speaker stood frozen and whispered, suggesting fear, shock, or tension, not joy. Therefore, "Yippee!" is inappropriate in this context. A more suitable interjection that can be used is: "Oh no!" So, there is an error in the part in option a). Hence, option (a) is the answer. The correct sentence is: She stood frozen at the doorway and whispered, "Oh no! There's someone inside the house."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. It is astonishing to see Federer's and Nadal's record in the world of tennis.
questions.explanation: ·The error lies in Option (b). ·"Federer and Nadal's" should replace "Federer's and Nadal's". ·When possessive nouns are joined together, we add an apostrophe to the last name only. ·For example: o Ronaldo and Messi's record o Man and Wife's fight ·Therefore, the corrected form of the sentence is: It is astonishing to see Federer and Nadal's record in the world of tennis.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: A curate's egg
options: Something partly good but largely spoiled by its bad parts
A situation that improves only temporarily
questions.explanation: "A curate's egg" refers to something with a few good features but overall rendered bad by its undesirable parts. Option (c) expresses this mixture of partial merit overshadowed by flaws. Options (a), (b), and (d) misrepresent the idiom's emphasis on inconsistent quality. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that depicts the central theme of the passage.
options: The tendency of humans to be drawn to negativity and its impact on our thoughts and emotions.
Humans are inherently positive beings, and negativity is an external force imposed upon us.
Negativity serves a necessary purpose in keeping us grounded and realistic.
The prevalence of negativity is due to societal conditioning rather than innate human tendencies.
questions.explanation: The passage is about why humans tend to focus on negativity and how it affects our thoughts and emotions. It explores why we sometimes find ourselves drawn to negative thoughts, even when they don't help us. It suggests that negativity can overshadow our happiness and talks about ways to break free from this pattern, like focusing on gratitude. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the closest synonym for "entangled" as used in the line, "It's curious how easily we can become entangled in the web of negativity, allowing it to seep into our thoughts and perceptions like a slow-acting poison"?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (c). In this context, "entangled" describes becoming caught, trapped, or deeply involved in negativity, much like being caught in a web. The word "ensnared" conveys this same sense of being trapped or held tightly by something harmful or difficult to escape, making it the closest synonym. Option (a) Liberated is incorrect because it suggests freedom, the opposite of being caught. Option (b) Distanced is incorrect since it means moved away or kept apart, which contradicts being pulled into negativity. Option (d) Detached is incorrect because it indicates emotional separation, not entrapment. Therefore, "ensnared" best matches the meaning of "entangled" in this sentence.

questions.question: What grammatical function does the phrase "to rob us of our joy and contentment" serve in the sentence? "And yet, despite its pervasiveness, negativity ultimately serves no purpose other than to rob us of our joy and contentment."
options: Noun phrase functioning as a complement
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (c). The phrase "to rob us of our joy and contentment" is an infinitival noun phrase functioning as a complement to the noun "purpose." It completes the meaning of "purpose" by specifying what the purpose isnamely, the act of robbing us of joy and contentment. Option (a) is incorrect because the phrase does not explain a cause; it expresses the content of the "purpose." Option (b) is incorrect because it does not function adjectivally; rather than describing "purpose," it completes its meaning. Option (d) is incorrect because the phrase is not a verb phrase showing action in progress; it is an infinitive phrase functioning nominally. Thus, the phrase serves as a noun complement to "purpose."

questions.question: Which of these things is NOT a good idea for getting rid of negativity?
options: Being thankful for little things that make you happy.
Thinking a lot about the times you didn't do well.
Paying attention to what's good instead of what's bad.
Believing that negativity is just how life is normally.
questions.explanation: According to the passage, "Perhaps part of the problem lies in our tendency to dwell on the negative aspects of our lives, allowing them to overshadow the positive moments like a dark cloud obscuring the sun." So, dwelling on past failures and shortcomings can contribute to negativity rather than alleviating it. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most suitable title of the passage.
questions.explanation: The passage talks about why people often feel negative and how to deal with it. It explains why we tend to focus on the bad things and suggests ways to change that. So, "Understanding and Overcoming Negativity" is the best title because it captures what the passage is all about. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the sentence which gives the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. A blessing in disguise
options: Losing his old job initially seemed terrible, but it led him to start his own successful business.
The unexpected holiday was a welcome opportunity for everyone to relax and spend time with family.
The sudden storm caused significant damage to the crops just a week before the harvest.
He received a small, unexpected bonus from his company for his consistent hard work and dedication.
questions.explanation: The idiom "a blessing in disguise" refers to something that seems bad or unlucky at first but results in something good later on. Option (a): This sentence perfectly illustrates the idiom: a seemingly negative event (losing a job) led to a positive outcome (starting a successful business). Correct. Option (b): This describes an event that was good from the start, not something that initially seemed bad. Incorrect. Option (c): This describes an event that was simply bad with no mention of a positive outcome. Incorrect. Option (d): This describes a small, positive event, not one that was disguised as a problem. Incorrect. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The accountant has finalized the budget report for this quarter.
options: The budget report is finalized by the accountant for this quarter.
The budget report has been finalized by the accountant for this quarter.
The budget report was being finalized by the accountant for this quarter.
The budget report had been finalized by the accountant for this quarter.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Present Perfect. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Present Perfect. The format of Active voice is: Subject + has/have + V3 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object + has/have + been + V3 + by + Subject. For example: Arya has bought a new horse. (Active Voice) A new horse has been bought by Arya. (Passive Voice) The options except (b) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (b): The budget report has been finalized by the accountant for this quarter. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. She sat by the window, her pensive gaze lost in the memories of distant days.
questions.explanation: Pensive: Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought. Helpful: Giving or ready to give help; useful. Dutiful: Conscientiously or obediently fulfilling one's duty. Thoughtful: Absorbed in or involving thought; showing consideration for others. Friendly: Kind and pleasant; amicable. In the sentence, "pensive" describes the state of someone who is lost in thought, specifically reflecting deep or serious thoughts. The most appropriate synonym from the given options is "thoughtful". Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correctly spelt word. The committee criticised his ___ remarks, which showed a lack of sensitivity towards marginalised communities.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, flippant is correctly spelt and means not showing due seriousness or sensitivity, which suits the context of insensitive remarks. Hence, (d) flippant is the correct answer. The other options contain spelling errors.

questions.question: The figure of speech used in "Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers" is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (d) Alliteration. Alliteration is a figure of speech that involves the repetition of the same initial consonant sound in closely positioned words. In this sentence, the repetition of the 'p' sound in Peter, Piper, picked, peck, pickled, peppers creates a rhythmic and musical effect, enhancing the line's appeal. Irony (option a) expresses the opposite of what is meant, often to create humor or emphasize contrast, which is not present here. Paradox (option b) combines seemingly contradictory ideas that reveal a deeper truth (e.g., "The beginning of the end"), which does not apply in this tongue-twister. Epigram (option c) is a concise, witty, and often paradoxical statement (e.g., "The child is the father of the man"), which also is not applicable here.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Mundane
questions.explanation: 'Mundane' means '(Adj) Lacking interest or excitement'. Options b) and d) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Dull' means 'Lacking interest or excitement'. Hence, option a) can also be rejected as it has the same meaning as the word. 'Maverick' means 'An unorthodox or independent-minded person'. 'Jettison' means 'Throw or drop (something) from an aircraft or ship'. Hence, option c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A sentence, split into four parts, has been given. Choose the best order which produces the original sentence. but it was my bad luck(A)/ I had prepared really well(B)/ and felt it was my day(C)/ with the rain pouring(D)
questions.explanation: The correct order is BCAD. Part B introduces the speaker's action of thorough preparation, emphasizing their readiness for something. It sets the stage by establishing the speaker's state of preparedness. Part C follows the previous part to express the speaker's positive mindset or confidence. It indicates that they strongly believed the specific day would be advantageous or successful for them. Part A introduces a contrasting element or unexpected outcome. It reveals that despite the speaker's confidence and preparation, unfortunate circumstances arose. Part D specifies the unfortunate circumstance or event that happened. It highlights the pouring rain as the factor that negatively affected the speaker's expectations or plans. By arranging these parts in the given order, the sentence forms a coherent and meaningful statement. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. The rearranged sentence is: I had prepared really well and felt it was my day but it was my bad luck with the rain pouring.

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in the correct order. P. Defined our community Q. Ongoing turmoil is that we appear to be R. One of the tragedies of the S. Abandoning the remarkable unity that once
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a meaningful sentence is RQSP. Part R introduces the main idea of the sentence, which is the tragedy associated with something. Part Q comes next as it specifies what the tragedy is related to (it is related to an ongoing turmoil). Accordingly, RQ forms a necessary pair. It suggests that due to the turmoil, there is a perception or likelihood of something happening. Parts S and P then elaborate on what action is likely to happen as a result of the ongoing turmoil. These parts state that there is a risk of abandoning the remarkable unity that the community once had. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: One of the tragedies of the ongoing turmoil is that we appear to be abandoning the remarkable unity that once defined our community.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Lack of feeling.
questions.explanation: 'Apathy' means lack of feeling. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. 'Sympathy' means feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune. 'Noxious' means harmful, poisonous, or very unpleasant. 'Cavalry' means (in the past) soldiers who fought on horseback.
